hypothesis: If a conditional latent diffusion model trained on the CIAE latent space is conditioned on adsorbate identity, adsorption energy, and allowed element lists, then it will generate shell-wise catalytic surface compositions that more closely match the real data distribution (lower Fréchet Distance), better satisfy chemical constraints (lower Leakage), and exhibit higher point-wise similarity to ground-truth configurations (higher Cosine Similarity) than a chemically-agnostic baseline, because conditioning provides explicit chemical context that guides generation toward chemically feasible and relevant surfaces.
